messages.content:
CVE-2022-25026 describes a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in Rocket TRUfusion Portal version 7.9.2.1. This vulnerability allows remote attackers to compromise internal network resources by sending crafted HTTP requests to the /trufusionPortal/upDwModuleProxy endpoint. The flaw is categorized under CWE-918, indicating a lack of proper destination assurance for URL requests processed by the server.

The severity of this vulnerability is rated as HIGH due to its potential impact on sensitive internal network components. The exploitation of such vulnerabilities may facilitate unauthorized access and data breaches, especially when an attacker can manipulate the requests to target critical back-end systems.

The affected software includes Rocket TRUfusion Enterprise products with versions prior to 7.9.5.1. Organizations using this software should implement mitigation techniques such as network segmentation and strict input validation (referenced as M1048, M1037, M1030, etc.), aligning with the associated MITRE ATT&CK technique T1190. Prompt application of vendor patches and adherence to security best practices are recommended to mitigate risks associated with this vulnerability.
